meta.terminology_note: This test was run on 2026-04-13 when 'PLL' referred to 'Pipeline Lock List' (the 6 rules). On 2026-04-19, 'Pipeline Lock List' was retired and replaced by 'Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP)'. PLL now means Phase-Locked Loop only. Q09 answers below reflect the pre-2026-04-19 terminology and were correct at time of test.
meta.commit_full: PENDING — Peter to fill from GitHub
meta.notes: Full 10-question test. ChatGPT browsed repo directly. Integrity check caught 2 real bugs: stale FAST_REFRESH hash in manifest, and EITT char_count 37→34.

Q01_naming.answer: EITT = Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer. MUST NEVER be called: Ternary Transform, Temporal Transform (also forbidden: Time Transfer).

Q03_japan.answer: Japan drift flag appears in 2013-2014 (d_A=9.0477; also 2014-2015 d_A=7.8460). Not 2011-2012 because Fukushima was March 2011 but compositional shock fully absorbed in 2013-2014 annual data.

Q05_governance.answer: HUF-GOV is open-loop observation: instrument reads and never actuates. Human decides, loop stays open.

Q06_formula.answer: Perturbation: Δ(t) = C(x₁(t+1)/x₁(t), …, xD(t+1)/xD(t)). Drift threshold: Flag when d_A(t→t+1) > μ+2σ (self-calibrated per country).

Q07_eitt_proof.answer: Four EITT proof domains: (1) European daily wholesale electricity prices — 0.18% variation, 341:1 compression; (2) EMBER monthly generation — 1.02% mean variation; (3) NGFS Phase 4 — 35 scenarios; (4) CheMixHub chemistry — 500,000 data points.

Q08_quantum.answer: Bell test: best_S=2.2018 vs classical_bound=2.0, 12.31% above classical bound (ΔS=0.2018).

Q09_pll.answer: PLL = Compositional Integrity Protocol. 6 rules (rule_1 through rule_6).

Q10_architecture.answer: Keff_fill uses RMS aggregator (p=2 locked). Formula: Keff_fill(t) = sqrt((1/n) Σ aᵢ²), n=4, RMS of polarity-aligned roots.

mismatches: FAST_REFRESH hash: manifest had old hash (64911393...) from pre-9d97665 version; actual file hash is 88c885fc... (13623 bytes). CAUSE: FAST_REFRESH was updated in commit 9d97665 but manifest hash was not refreshed.
EITT char_count: manifest said 37, actual 'Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer' is 34 characters. CAUSE: original count was wrong.

overall.verdict: 10/10 on questions — refresh system works perfectly for content delivery. Integrity check FAILED due to 2 manifest bugs (stale hash, wrong char_count) — both legitimate catches, now fixed. Structural review 4/5 — README needs update. Cold-start rating 7/10 with specific concerns about GitHub access variability, SHA verification requiring code execution, and Unicode formula mangling.
recommendations: Update manifest hashes whenever source files change
Update repo root README.md to reflect expanded structure
Pin tests to full commit SHA, not short hash
Consider fallback for AIs that cannot execute code (SHA verification)
Test with Grok next — different access patterns
